prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in this quarter most peculiar, the scribes of the digital realm have tallied their gains in units most ethereal—"annual recurring revenue," they call it, a mystical measurement that doth baffle my comprehension. The noble lords of this enterprise, clad not in armor but in garments of cotton and strange devices of illumination, speak of "user acquisition" as though summoning peasants to their virtual castle. Their leader, a bearded sage who communicates through missives called "Slack" and bears the title of "Chief Executive Officer," hath proclaimed great triumph over competitors in lands unknown, mapped only by lines of arcane code. I know not whether to record these proceedings with my quill or to cast protective charms against the sorcery of spreadsheets and cloud-dwelling ledgers.
